At approximately 14:45, during the ongoing production run of product L49195 variant L, the cell HMI indicated a sudden spike in tool wear, reaching 42 minutes on the gauge, while the rotational speed was steady at 1301 rpm. I observed a slight vibration emanating from the machine and noticed a faint, acrid smell in the area. The air temperature was reading 298.5 K, and the process temperature was 308.2 K, with a delta temperature of 9.7 K. On my visual inspection, the part exiting the machine appeared to be slightly deformed. I immediately stopped the line and called maintenance to investigate further, as the torque reading was 66.3 Nm.

Likely cause: Drivetrain power above 9000 W (9033 W) — overload from high torque (66.3 Nm) at 1301 rpm. Immediate action: Stop the spindle immediately and clear any chips or jam at the cutting zone."}
